prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
# The Last Brushstroke

After months of doubt and midnight oil,
The scaffolding dismantled, piece by piece,
I stand back from this monument of toil
And feel the strange approaching of release.

My shoulders drop their burden to the floor;
My mind, so cluttered, starts to clear and drain.
The space that once held only this—no more—
Now echoes with a gentle, hollow gain.

What strange relief, this ending and this birth,
To watch my labors finally take flight.
The work complete, I measure now its worth
In sudden time returned, in new daylight.
